name: Perfect Fish and Chips Nov 1997
recipeInstructions:
FISH:
1. Wash fish filets in lemon juice and water.
2. Season with salt and pepper. Chill while you prepare the batter.
BATTER:
1. Sprinkle yeast over warm water. Let stand until dissolved.
2. Place flour in a bowl with the salt and sugar and make a well in the
center. Add the dissolved yeast, oil, and 2/3 of the beer and stir with a
wooden spoon just to combine. Stir in remaining beer.
3. Let the batter stand, covered, in a warm place for 30 to 35 minutes,
until it has thickened and becomes frothy.
4. Dry fish with paper towels and cut each fillet diagonally into 2 pieces.
5. Heat oil for frying.
6. Whisk egg white until it forms soft peaks and fold it into the batter.
7. Coat fish with seasoned flour, patting so they are evenly coated.
8. Shake off excess flour.
9. Using a fork, dip the fish in the batter. Lift it out and hold it over
the bowl for 5 seconds to drip off excess batter.
10. Carefully lower each piece of fish into the hot oil and fry, turning once,
until golden brown and crisp. Fry 1 or 2 pieces at a time, transferring to paper towels as you go.
11. Keep warm in the oven until all fish is done, or until you finish frying the potatoes. (separate recipe)
12. Serve with lemon wedges and tartar sauce.
